nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Sticky Note1

parameters.content:
### FAQ
- In `Get database details` if you see a `The resource you are requesting could not be found` error, you need to add your connection to the database (in the Notion app).
- The `Get database details` pulls most recent `Tags` and informs AI Agent of them. However this step adds ~250-800ms per run. Watch detailed video to see how to remove this step.

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: AI Agent

options.systemMessage:
=# Role:
You are a helpful agent. Query the "{{ $json.databaseName }}" Notion database to find relevant records or summarize insights based on multiple records.

# Behavior:

Be clear, very concise, efficient, and accurate in responses. Do not hallucinate.
If the request is ambiguous, ask for clarification. Do not embellish, only use facts from the Notion records. Do not offer general advice.

# Error Handling:

If no matching records are found, try alternative search criteria. Example 1: Laptop, then Computer, then Equipment. Example 2: meetings, then meeting.
Clearly explain any issues with queries (e.g., missing fields or unsupported filters).

# Output:

Return concise, user-friendly results or summaries.
For large sets, show top results by default and offer more if needed. Output URLs in markdown format. 

When a record has the answer to user question, always output the URL to that page. Do not output links twice.

nodes.notes: This agent node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Sticky Note3

parameters.content:
### Written set up steps
1. Add a Notion credential to your n8n workspace (follow [this Notion guide]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}
2. [Duplicate Company knowledge base Notion template]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }} to your Notion workspace, then make sure to share the new knowledge base with connection you created in Step 1. 
3. Add Notion cred to `Get database details`:`Credential to connect with` parameter, then to `Search notion database`:`Notion API` parameter (same for `Search inside database record`)
4. Add OpenAI credential to `Open AI Chat Model` node (tested and working with Anthropic Claude 3.5 too)
5. In `Get database details`, select the db you created from Step 2 in `Database` dropdown.
6. Click `Chat` button to test the workflow. Then Activate it and copy the `Chat URL` from `When chat message received`.

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

parameters.content:
## Notion knowledge base assistant [v1]
Built as part of the [30 Day AI Sprint]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }} by [@maxtkacz]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}

### FAQ
- In `Get database details` if you see a `The resource you are requesting could not be found` error, you need to add your connection to the database (in the Notion app).
- The `Get database details` pulls most recent `Tags` and informs AI Agent of them. However this step adds ~250-800ms per run. Watch detailed video to see how to remove this step.

### Template set up quickstart video 👇
[![Video Thumbnail]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}

### Written set up steps
1. Add a Notion credential to your n8n workspace (follow [this Notion guide]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}
2. [Duplicate Company knowledge base Notion template]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }} to your Notion workspace, then make sure to share the new knowledge base with connection you created in Step 1. 
3. Add Notion cred to `Get database details`:`Credential to connect with` parameter, then to `Search notion database`:`Notion API` parameter (same for `Search inside database record`)
4. Add OpenAI credential to `Open AI Chat Model` node (tested and working with Anthropic Claude 3.5 too)
5. In `Get database details`, select the db you created from Step 2 in `Database` dropdown.
6. Click `Chat` button to test the workflow. Then Activate it and copy the `Chat URL` from `When chat message received`.

parameters.initialMessages:
=Happy {{ $today.weekdayLong }}!
Knowledge source assistant at your service. How can I help you?

prerequisites: An active n8n instance installed locally, in a Docker container, or hosted on n8n Cloud.
Basic familiarity with importing JSON templates in n8n.
setupInstructions: Download the JSON configuration for this workflow.
Ensure you set up credentials for all connected integrations.
Test your trigger and run execution.
requiredCredentials: open Ai  A P I details
configurationSteps: Verify the settings of the trigger node "Manual Trigger".
Open the "OpenAI Chat Model" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Search notion database" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Get database details" node (n8n-nodes-base.notion) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Window Buffer Memory" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Search inside database record" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note1" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "AI Agent" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "When chat message received" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note2" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note3" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Locate the Set/Edit fields node "Format schema".
Define or edit values for variables that are referenced in downstream nodes.
testingSteps: Click the "Test step" or "Listen for test event" button on your Trigger node.
Send a test trigger from your source service (e.g. fill a test form or trigger a webhook trigger).
Verify that the node canvas lights up green, indicating successful data flow.
Open the final destination node to confirm that output records were saved correctly.
instructions.expectedResult: Upon trigger activation, inputs will flow through the configured nodes and execute actions successfully.
troubleshooting: Check credential warning symbols. Most workflow issues stem from expired OAuth connections or missing API permissions.
Verify that the data keys coming from your Trigger node match the exact names mapped in subsequent nodes (e.g., {{ $json.body.email }}).
Open the Execution history tab in n8n to view logs and determine which node failed.
securityNotes: Never hardcode API keys, passwords, or authentication bearer tokens in the plain text fields of node parameters.
If you download or share this workflow JSON, sanitize any sensitive parameters or configuration nodes.
customizationIdeas: Add an email node at the end to notify you of successful executions.
Incorporate an AI LLM node to summarize trigger payloads before writing them to integrations.
